message: subprocess.run(command, shell=True) with HTTP request parameter 'command' and no authentication dependency. Unauthenticated OS command injection (RCE).
entry_point: EP-001 (POST /api/shell, auth=false)

message: When OSTWIN_API_KEY=DEBUG all authentication checks are bypassed. X-User header can impersonate any identity.
entry_point: ALL-AUTHENTICATED-ENDPOINTS (any HTTP request when DEBUG key set)

message: CORSMiddleware configured with allow_origins=['*']. Combined with cookie auth enables cross-origin credential theft and drive-by exploitation.
entry_point: Any authenticated endpoint (browser cross-origin request); SK-002 present in sinks.json

message: POST /api/plans/create has no Depends(get_current_user). Unauthenticated attackers can write arbitrary files to ~/.ostwin/plans/.
entry_point: EP-007 (POST /api/plans/create, auth=false)

message: Hardcoded fallback encryption key 'ostwin-default-insecure-key-32ch'. Any attacker with vault file access can decrypt all stored secrets.
entry_point: EP-INTERNAL (EncryptedFileVault init; vault file reachable post-exploitation or via path traversal)

message: Discord message.content flows unsanitized into Gemini generateContent prompt. Enables prompt injection, context exfiltration, and output hijacking.
entry_point: EP-021 (Discord messageCreate event, auth=true via Discord server membership)

message: GET /api/run_pytest_auth has no authentication and spawns pytest subprocess. Unauthenticated subprocess execution with stdout/stderr disclosure.
entry_point: EP-002 (GET /api/run_pytest_auth, auth=false)

message: GET /api/test_ws has no authentication and spawns subprocess. Unauthenticated subprocess execution.
entry_point: EP-003 (GET /api/test_ws, auth=false)

message: GET /api/telegram/config has no authentication. Exposes bot token and chat ID to unauthenticated callers.
entry_point: EP-004 (GET /api/telegram/config, auth=false)

message: POST /api/telegram/config has no authentication. Allows overwriting Telegram bot credentials.
entry_point: EP-005 (POST /api/telegram/config, auth=false)

message: POST /api/telegram/test has no authentication. Allows sending arbitrary Telegram messages.
entry_point: EP-006 (POST /api/telegram/test, auth=false)

message: Raw API key (_API_KEY) returned in JSON response body as access_token. Key exposed in browser devtools, proxy logs, and JavaScript memory.
entry_point: EP-LOGIN (POST /api/auth/token; any caller who authenticates receives the raw key)
codeql_slice: no-dedicated-slice (SK-004 confirmed in sinks.json, login path confirmed)

message: DASHBOARD_URL read from process.env without scheme/host validation. Attacker-controlled env redirects all API calls (with X-API-Key) to arbitrary hosts (SSRF).
entry_point: EP-022 (DASHBOARD_URL env var; requires attacker control of process environment)
codeql_slice: no-dedicated-slice (SK-010 in sinks.json but source is env var requiring privileged position)
drop_reason: Requires attacker control of the deployment environment (process.env). This is a privileged position equivalent to code execution. Classified as environment/admin-only; does not cross an exploitable network trust boundary from an unauthenticated external attacker.

message: Session cookie set without secure=True. The API key cookie is transmitted in plaintext over HTTP connections.
entry_point: EP-LOGIN (POST /api/auth/token; cookie issued without secure flag, interceptable on any HTTP connection)

message: WebSocket /api/ws has no authentication. Any client can connect and receive real-time agent event broadcasts.
entry_point: EP-020 (WS /api/ws, auth=false)

message: POST /api/plans/{id}/status has no authentication. Unauthenticated plan status mutation.
entry_point: EP-008 (POST /api/plans/{id}/status, auth=false)

message: POST /api/plans/refine has no authentication. Unauthenticated LLM prompt injection via refine endpoint.
entry_point: EP-010 (POST /api/plans/refine, auth=false)

message: GET /api/events SSE endpoint has no authentication. Internal agent events broadcast to unauthenticated clients.
entry_point: EP-015 (GET /api/events, auth=false)

message: POST /api/rooms/{id}/action has no authentication. Unauthenticated room state mutation (stop/pause/resume).
entry_point: EP-019 (POST /api/rooms/{id}/action, auth=false)

message: GET /api/fs/browse?path= passes user-supplied path to Path().expanduser().resolve() without base-directory bounds check. Directory traversal to any readable path.
entry_point: No auth on /api/fs/browse (system.py unauthenticated routes cluster); SK-011 tainted=true in sinks.json

message: User-supplied plan_id/room_id values flow into Path() construction across api_utils.py without sanitization. Multiple path traversal sinks.
entry_point: EP-007/EP-008 (plan/room endpoints passing user-controlled IDs into path helpers)
codeql_slice: no-dedicated-slice (taint flows confirmed via SK-008/SK-012 in sinks.json and unauthenticated entry points)

message: room_id from HTTP path parameter flows into filesystem path construction. Path traversal risk in room directory resolution.
entry_point: EP-017/EP-018/EP-019 (room endpoints with auth=false)

message: plan_id from HTTP path parameter flows into filesystem path construction without bounds validation.
entry_point: EP-012 (GET /api/plans/{id}/epics, auth=false)

message: User-supplied search query passed to re.match() without re.escape(). Enables regex injection and potential ReDoS.
entry_point: EP-013/EP-014 (GET /api/search/plans and /api/search/epics, auth=false)

message: Polynomial-time regular expression applied to user-provided value. Crafted input can cause catastrophic backtracking (DoS).
entry_point: Skills validate endpoint (POST /api/skills/validate, behind get_current_user — but auth is bypassable via SAST-002 DEBUG bypass or exploitable by any valid user causing shared-server DoS)
codeql_slice: no-dedicated-slice (skills.py endpoints confirmed to have auth dependency, but auth is bypassable; any authenticated user can trigger ReDoS affecting all users)

message: Polynomial-time regular expression applied to user-provided value. Crafted input can cause catastrophic backtracking (DoS).
entry_point: Skills validate endpoint (POST /api/skills/validate, behind get_current_user — bypassable via SAST-002 or triggerable by any authenticated user)

message: Stack trace information flows to API response and may be exposed to external users. Leaks internal file paths and module structure.
entry_point: MCP routes (triggerable by any caller who can provoke an exception on the endpoint, including unauthenticated paths)
codeql_slice: no-dedicated-slice (pattern confirmed via SAST narrative; CWE-209 cross-cutting across mcp.py)

message: Stack trace information flows to API response and may be exposed to external users.
entry_point: MCP routes (same as SAST-017a)

message: Stack trace information flows to API response and may be exposed to external users.
entry_point: Plans routes (multiple unauthenticated plan endpoints per entry-points.json)

message: Stack trace information flows to API response and may be exposed to external users.
entry_point: Roles routes (reachable to any authenticated user; auth bypassable via SAST-002)

message: Sensitive secret value logged via standard logging module. Log files may be accessible to unauthorized parties.
entry_point: None (log file read requires privileged/admin access to the host filesystem)
drop_reason: Exploiting this finding requires read access to log files on the host, which is a privileged/admin-only position. The attacker cannot trigger exposure via a network-facing entry point. Classified as environment (admin-only); does not cross an attacker-accessible trust boundary.

message: verify_password() always returns True regardless of input. Any future invocation will accept arbitrary passwords (dormant vulnerability).
entry_point: None (function is dormant; not currently called in any active authentication path per SAST narrative)
codeql_slice: no-dedicated-slice (SK-007 in sinks.json marked tainted=false; dormant code path)
drop_reason: LOW severity — mandatory drop per Phase 7 drop criteria. Additionally the function is dormant (not invoked in active auth path), making this a correctness/future-risk issue rather than a presently exploitable vulnerability.
